slides.title: Your brain runs a *wash cycle* while you sleep.
slides.sub: Deep sleep is when it cleans itself. Protecting it may protect your mind for decades.

slides.title: An overnight rinse
slides.body: In deep sleep your brain opens a drainage system, the glymphatic system, and *flushes out waste* it collected all day.

slides.title: It clears the residue
slides.body: That rinse removes waste proteins that are *linked to cognitive decline*. Skipping deep sleep leaves more of them behind.

slides.title: Short sleep adds up
slides.body: Chronically short nights are *linked to faster brain aging*. The cost is quiet, building over years rather than days.

slides.title: Set the room first
slides.body: Protect deep sleep with a *cool, dark room* and no late alcohol or caffeine. Both can blunt your deepest stages.

slides.title: The early hours win
slides.body: Deep sleep clusters *early in the night*. Going to bed on time matters more than sleeping in to catch up.

slides.title: Protect the sleep that *clears your mind*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your sleep alongside food, water and steps, so the nights that protect your brain show up in one number.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Deep sleep is when your brain runs an overnight wash cycle, clearing out the waste it built up all day. 🌙

It removes proteins linked to cognitive decline, so a cool dark room and no late alcohol or caffeine are worth protecting. The deep sleep early in the night matters most. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your sleep into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How Deep Sleep Cleans Your Brain Every Night
